meaning: adj.海滨的,沿海的n.沿海地,沿海地区
examples: New ships, including a destroyer, a littoral combat ship and an ocean surveillance ship
Indeed before the age of tourism not many littoral folk learned to swim; the risk of drowning was too high.
Originally part of the Army's Future Combat Systems program, NLOS-LS also is intended for the Navy's Littoral Combat Ship.
Purchasing two very different versions of the Littoral Combat Ship may increase operational costs.
So the littoral warship costs much less to build and operate than other warships in the fleet.
